venue: Estadio Azteca, Mexico City (quasi-home for Mexico)

qualification_math.Czechia: WIN = 4pts, enters third-place contention (8 of 12 advance). Draw or loss = eliminated.
qualification_math.Mexico: Already through. Playing for fun/fitness. Rotating aggressively for R32.

group_context.venue_note: Estadio Azteca is Mexico's home ground. Crowd will be 80-90% pro-Mexico. Not in Elo model (neutral=TRUE) but real psychological/tactical effect.

cze_gd1_vs_kor.schick: played 64 min, 5.9/10 FotMob, poor performance, substituted off

Patrik_Schick.gd3_expected: START — CZE's main striker, will be relied upon in must-win

Adam_Hlozek.note: Co-striker. Market: Over 0.5 SOT at -132 (57% implied) — competes with Schick for shots

Mexico_rotation.Rangel_status: No.1 GK being rested for knockouts
Mexico_rotation.starters_expected: Santiago Gimenez, Alvarado, Romo, Huerta, Galindo rotation squad

referee.name: Yael Falcón Pérez
referee.card_rate_avg: Not confirmed — flag for future reference
referee.notes: Female referee. Argentina national referees program.

rules_applied.RULE8: FIRES — draw 27.57% ≥ 25%. Shrink extreme attacking props toward 50%. Cagier match expected. MEX-KOR GD2 precedent: 1-0, RULE8 fully confirmed.
rules_applied.RULE_FOULS: CZE is underdog, defends → fouls more. Q1 direction = YES.
rules_applied.FLOOR_0.25: Not applied (Q3 estimate at 0.28, above floor)
rules_applied.AZTECA_EFFECT: Quasi-home for Mexico. Slight downward on CZE win from what rotation alone would suggest.
rules_applied.ROTATION_EFFECT: MEX resting Jimenez/Quinones → CZE win prob UP from model. MEX attacking stats (offsides, corners) DOWN slightly.

Q1.question: Will Czechia commit more fouls than Mexico?
Q1.rationale: RULE_FOULS: CZE is underdog (18.5% win), defends more, fouls more. Even with MEX rotating, they control the ball and CZE chases.

Q2.question: Will Mexico be caught offside 2 or more times?
Q2.rationale: Model 0.845, RULE8 shrink (cagier match). MEX-KOR GD2: Mexico DID have 2+ offsides. Rotation slightly reduces coordinated forward runs.

Q3.question: Will Czechia have more shots on target than Mexico in the second half?
Q3.rationale: Model 0.189. CZE survival uplift and MEX rotation offset, but MEX still λ_sot 4.598 vs CZE 2.410. Strong NO. BEST EDGE in CZE-MEX.

Q4.question: Will Mexico finish with more corner kicks than Czechia?
Q4.rationale: Model 0.765, RULE8. Key evidence: in MEX-KOR, KOR got MORE corners than Mexico — corner dominance is volatile. Nearly coin-flip here.

Q5.question: Will both teams score AND the match have 3 or more total goals?
Q5.rationale: Model 0.314, RULE8. MEX-KOR (similar Elo gap): 1-0 only goal, RULE8 fully confirmed. Slight upward from rotation opening match.

Q6.question: Will Czechia win the match?
Q6.rationale: Model 0.185. MEX rotating heavily = big uplift. Azteca crowd offsets some of rotation advantage. Crowd will also price rotation → crowd at ~0.40.

Q7.question: Will Czechia score in the second half?
Q7.rationale: Model 0.331. CZE survival = they push in 2H. Krejčí scored in 59' (2H) vs KOR in GD1. MEX rotation = gaps in defense. Some uplift but still NO_LEAN.

Q8.question: Will there be 4 or more total cards shown?
Q8.rationale: Referee Falcón Pérez: Argentina, no specific card rate confirmed. CZE desperation = physical play = cards. MEX rotation = calmer. Slight NO lean pending ref data.

Q9.question: Will Patrik Schick have at least 1 shot on target?
Q9.rationale: Model 0.570, but Schick has 1 SOT in 154 WC minutes. Hlozek also shooting. RULE8 reduces overall shots. CZE needs win = he'll be relied on. Coin-flip with slight NO lean.

post_match_results.note_submission_error: Q9 SUBMISSION ERROR: Platform shows 0.72 submitted for Schick SOT, but our pre-match JSON estimate was 0.48. Believed to be accidental submission of RSA-KOR Son estimate (0.72) for the wrong question. Outcome was NO, RBP = -25.42 (catastrophic). Correct estimate 0.48 would have earned positive RBP.

Q9.question: Schick 1+ SOT
Q9.ERROR: SUBMISSION ERROR — intended 0.48, accidentally submitted 0.72 (Son RSA-KOR estimate). At 0.48, outcome NO would have yielded positive RBP beating crowd's 0.47.

key_learnings: SUBMISSION ERROR (Q9): Schick SOT estimate 0.48 accidentally submitted as 0.72 (Son's RSA-KOR estimate). Cost approximately -30 RBP swing vs what 0.48 would have earned. Double-check cross-match estimates before submitting.
CZE 0-3 MEX — Mexico rotation was no weakness. Santiago Gimenez and rotation squad scored 3. Rotation effect may have been less severe than assumed.
Q1 (CZE more fouls) = NO despite RULE_FOULS — Mexico fouled more in a 3-0 win. RULE10 analog: winning team presses/fouls more in a dominant win, even if they were expected to foul less.
Q2 (MEX 2+ offsides) = NO despite rotation — Mexico's offense was disciplined, not caught offside. Rotation reduced forward coordination as expected, but fell below even our reduced 0.58 estimate.

Q4 (MEX more corners) = NO in a 3-0 Mexico win — THIRD consecutive match where the winning team had FEWER corners (after MEX-KOR, SCO-BRA). Corners are genuinely not predictable from dominance. Our near-coin-flip (0.52) was correct posture.
Q3, Q5, Q6, Q7, Q8 all beat crowd (5 structural NO edges all paid off) — the core model NO-lean estimates for CZE were correct.
